course.title: From the Transistor to the Web Browser
course.description: A cumulative, software-first systems course that grows a simulated computer from bits and gates into a small Unix-like system with a text web browser.
course.audience: Programmers new to C and computer construction, including learners with some reverse-engineering experience

toolchain.purpose: Run the course CLI, reference models, assemblers, and test harnesses
toolchain.install_hint: Install Python 3.11 or newer from your operating system package manager

toolchain.purpose: Provide stable build and check entry points across labs
toolchain.install_hint: Install GNU Make or a compatible make implementation

toolchain.purpose: Compile the C runway exercises and host-side test programs
toolchain.install_hint: Install Clang or GCC and ensure the cc command is available

toolchain.purpose: Checkpoint learner work and inspect changes safely
toolchain.install_hint: Install Git from your operating system package manager

toolchain.purpose: Compile and trace the optional synthesizable RTL path
toolchain.install_hint: Install Verilator 5 or newer when beginning the RTL extension

toolchain.purpose: Inspect ELF headers, sections, symbols, and relocations
Disassemble and investigate host and target object files
Explore the optional cumulative notebooks interactively after the offline checker passes
toolchain.install_hint: Install the notebooks optional dependency group

sections.title: C Runway
sections.summary: Build the C, byte-level, debugging, ABI, and digital-logic foundation needed by every cumulative project.

sections.title: Intro
sections.summary: Cross the abstraction boundary from transistor switches to LUTs, then establish a deterministic simulation workflow.

sections.title: Bringup
sections.summary: Learn synchronous hardware construction through an LED peripheral and a tested UART exposed through MMIO.

sections.title: Processor
sections.summary: Specify an ISA, assemble programs, execute them in a traceable CPU model, and load them through a boot ROM monitor.

sections.title: Compiler
sections.summary: Create a small C toolchain, freestanding runtime, packet device, and network-capable second-stage bootloader.

sections.title: Operating System
sections.summary: Add protected virtual memory, processes, system calls, persistent storage, a filesystem, and a usable shell.

sections.title: Browser
sections.summary: Build the networking and user-space components needed to fetch and render a small, deliberately bounded web page.

sections.title: Physical
sections.summary: Optionally carry the verified software machine onto supported FPGA hardware and investigate JTAG and board bringup.

modules.title: Course Orientation
modules.artifact: A verified local checkout, learning log, and written scope contract
modules.summary: Learn the cumulative machine, evidence standard, safe operating boundaries, and repeatable study loop.
objectives: Explain how each course section feeds the final browser
Run structural validation and distinguish it from a lab check
Create a recovery-friendly evidence and checkpoint routine

modules.title: Shell Build and Debug Loop
modules.path: vault/00 C Runway/00.02 Shell Build and Debug Loop.md
modules.artifact: A warning-clean program with a captured failing test, debugger trace, and repaired test
modules.summary: Practice a short edit-build-test-debug loop before the projects become layered.
objectives: Navigate and inspect files without destructive commands
Compile with strict diagnostics and interpret process status
Reduce a failure with tests, sanitizers, and a debugger

modules.title: C Values and Control Flow
modules.path: vault/00 C Runway/00.03 C Values and Control Flow.md
modules.artifact: A warning-clean command-line decoder with boundary-focused tests
modules.summary: Learn C expressions, integer types, functions, control flow, diagnostics, and the first forms of undefined behavior.
objectives: Trace C expression evaluation and control flow
Choose explicit-width integer types at machine boundaries
Turn input assumptions into checked preconditions and tests

modules.title: Bytes Bits and Representation
modules.path: vault/00 C Runway/00.04 Bytes Bits and Representation.md
modules.artifact: A byte-inspection utility that decodes fields without alignment or endianness assumptions
modules.summary: Make signedness, width, endianness, masks, shifts, and binary layouts observable rather than mysterious.
objectives: Convert between numeric values and byte representations
Extract and insert bit fields without undefined shifts
Recognize endianness and signedness mistakes from traces

modules.title: Pointers Arrays and Strings
modules.path: vault/00 C Runway/00.05 Pointers Arrays and Strings.md
modules.artifact: A bounded byte-buffer API with tests for empty, full, overlapping, and unterminated inputs
modules.summary: Treat pointers as typed addresses with explicit object bounds and lifetimes.
objectives: Draw pointer, array, and string object layouts
Write length-aware traversal and copying code
Diagnose out-of-bounds, use-after-lifetime, and missing-terminator faults

modules.title: Structs Memory and Ownership
modules.path: vault/00 C Runway/00.06 Structs Memory and Ownership.md
modules.artifact: A small owned collection with explicit init/destroy contracts and allocation-failure tests
modules.summary: Use structs and ownership conventions to make manual resource management reviewable.
objectives: Explain struct padding and why wire formats need explicit encoding
State ownership and lifetime rules at every API boundary
Clean up partially initialized objects on every failure path

modules.title: Functions ABI and Object Files
modules.path: vault/00 C Runway/00.07 Functions ABI and Object Files.md
modules.artifact: An annotated disassembly and object-file report for a multi-file C program
modules.summary: Connect C calls to registers, stack frames, symbols, sections, relocations, and final executables.
objectives: Trace arguments, return values, and saved state through a call
Distinguish compiling, assembling, linking, and loading
Use symbols and relocations to explain unresolved addresses

modules.title: Digital Logic and State Machines
modules.path: vault/00 C Runway/00.08 Digital Logic and State Machines.md
modules.artifact: A truth-table-derived combinational design and a cycle-accurate state-machine trace
modules.summary: Learn combinational logic, registers, clocks, and state machines before writing device RTL.
objectives: Derive logic from a truth table
Separate combinational next-state logic from registered state
Predict a synchronous machine one clock edge at a time

modules.title: Transistors Gates and LUTs
modules.path: vault/01 Intro/01.01 Transistors Gates and LUTs.md
modules.artifact: A tested two-input LUT and clocked wrapping counter plus an abstraction map from switch to FPGA LUT
modules.summary: Use transistor theory only far enough to justify gates, storage, LUTs, and the RTL abstraction boundary.
objectives: Explain what is preserved at each hardware abstraction layer
Map Boolean functions onto muxes and LUT contents
State why the course simulates rather than fabricates transistors

modules.title: Simulation and Waveforms
modules.artifact: A deterministic simulator harness with assertions, a failing seed, and an annotated waveform
modules.summary: Establish prediction, assertion, trace, and replay as the hardware debugging loop.
objectives: Distinguish simulation time, cycles, and host runtime
Write self-checking tests around observable interfaces
Reduce a failure using the first divergent waveform cycle

modules.title: Blinking an LED
modules.artifact: A resettable LED divider with period assertions and a short evidence waveform
modules.summary: Build the first visible synchronous peripheral without hiding timing errors behind a human-visible demo.
objectives: Implement a counter with explicit reset semantics
Derive toggle period from clock frequency
Test boundary cycles rather than merely viewing output

modules.title: UART and MMIO
modules.artifact: A tested UART transmit/receive state machine behind a documented MMIO register interface
modules.summary: Turn serial framing and a device register contract into the machine's first communication channel.
objectives: Trace UART frames and sample bits at defined cycles
Design MMIO status and data registers with explicit side effects
Recover from framing, overrun, and back-pressure conditions

modules.title: Assembler and ISA
modules.artifact: An exact FTT-16 assembler/disassembler with diagnostics, golden vectors, and an encoding notebook
modules.summary: Make the instruction encoding and ABI executable before depending on a processor implementation.
objectives: Encode instruction fields and signed immediates from the ISA table
Resolve labels without conflating source lines and byte addresses
Reject malformed programs with precise diagnostics

modules.title: CPU Emulator
modules.artifact: A bounded FTT-16 emulator with architectural traces, execution-budget failures, and an optional differential RTL core
modules.summary: Implement fetch, decode, execute, memory, and control transfer in a model that makes every state change inspectable.
objectives: State the precise architectural effect of each supported instruction
Enforce register-width, program-counter, and memory-boundary invariants
Find the first divergent state between two execution models

modules.title: Boot ROM Monitor
modules.artifact: A ROM monitor that validates, loads, inspects, and jumps to a RAM image through the simulated UART
modules.summary: Create a small trusted boot path and a recovery interface before adding higher-level software.
objectives: Design a byte-stream protocol that can resynchronize after bad input
Validate target ranges, lengths, and checksums before writes
Demonstrate a complete load-to-RAM-and-execute transcript

modules.title: C Compiler
modules.artifact: A compiler for a documented C subset with lexer, parser, typed AST, code generator, and differential tests
modules.summary: Translate a deliberately bounded C subset into target assembly while making language and ABI decisions explicit.
objectives: Separate syntax, type, and code-generation responsibilities
Lower expressions and control flow without losing evaluation rules
Test compiler behavior against source-level and machine-level oracles

modules.title: ELF Linker
modules.artifact: A static linker that lays out sections, resolves symbols, applies relocations, and emits inspectable ELF32 images
modules.summary: Replace raw binaries with a tested object-and-link model that supports separately compiled programs.
objectives: Explain sections, symbols, relocations, and segments
Compute relocation values with range and overflow checks
Diagnose duplicate, missing, and incompatible definitions

modules.title: Freestanding libc and Allocator
modules.path: vault/04 Compiler/04.03 Freestanding libc and Allocator.md
modules.artifact: A freestanding runtime with startup, memory/string routines, bounded formatting, and an invariant-checked allocator
modules.summary: Supply the small runtime contracts required by larger target C programs without pretending to implement all of libc.
objectives: Implement byte routines with overlap and boundary rules
Maintain allocator metadata invariants under split and coalesce
Separate target runtime services from host operating-system assumptions

modules.title: Ethernet Controller
modules.artifact: A deterministic MMIO packet device with bounded TX/RX queues, error tests, and PCAP evidence
modules.summary: Preserve the Ethernet/MMIO design lesson with a software packet backend before any real PHY work.
objectives: Define ownership transitions for transmit and receive buffers
Handle queue-full, truncation, and malformed-frame conditions
Correlate MMIO activity with captured Ethernet frames

modules.title: Network Bootloader
modules.artifact: A C second-stage loader with validated image framing, checksum, bounded placement, serial fallback, and boot transcript
modules.summary: Use the new C runtime and packet device to load kernels without replacing the recovery path.
objectives: Treat every image field and packet as hostile input
Validate an entire image before transferring control
Preserve a minimal serial recovery path

modules.title: MMU and Page Tables
modules.path: vault/05 Operating System/05.01 MMU and Page Tables.md
modules.artifact: A reference page-table walker with permission faults, TLB invalidation, and randomized translation tests
modules.summary: Extend the processor with coherent privilege, translation, permissions, and fault behavior before relying on isolation.
objectives: Trace a virtual address through page-table levels
Distinguish absent, malformed, and permission-denied mappings
Keep TLB state coherent when mappings change

modules.title: Unix-like Operating System
modules.path: vault/05 Operating System/05.02 Unix-like Operating System.md
modules.artifact: A kernel that boots userspace, isolates processes, implements the scoped syscall set, and records deterministic traces
modules.summary: Build the minimum coherent kernel around traps, memory, scheduling, processes, files, and ELF execution.
objectives: Maintain explicit invariants across traps and context switches
Validate all user pointers at the kernel boundary
Demonstrate fork, exec, wait, sleep, and protected mappings

modules.title: SD Card and Block Device
modules.path: vault/05 Operating System/05.03 SD Card and Block Device.md
modules.artifact: A sector device and driver backed by a disk image with timeout, bounds, and injected-I/O-failure tests
modules.summary: Keep the core storage lesson deterministic while isolating optional real SD signaling behind the same block API.
objectives: Define exact sector and buffer contracts
Propagate short and failed I/O without corrupting caller state
Separate filesystem policy from device mechanics

modules.title: FAT Filesystem
modules.artifact: A bounded FAT16 reader/writer whose disk image interoperates with host filesystem tools
modules.summary: Parse and mutate a narrow FAT16 profile without trusting on-disk offsets, lengths, or allocation chains.
objectives: Derive filesystem regions from checked boot-sector fields
Detect loops, invalid clusters, and truncated chains
Preserve consistency while creating, writing, and deleting files

modules.title: Init Shell and Utilities
modules.path: vault/05 Operating System/05.05 Init Shell and Utilities.md
modules.artifact: An init process, interactive shell, downloader, cat, ls, and rm with a replayable persistent-session transcript
modules.summary: Exercise process, file, terminal, and runtime contracts through the first usable target environment.
objectives: Launch and reap programs from a long-lived init process
Parse bounded command input and report syscall failures
Demonstrate filesystem state surviving a reboot

modules.title: TCP Stack
modules.artifact: A deliberately scoped ARP/IPv4/TCP stack with socket calls and deterministic loss/reordering tests
modules.summary: Add the omitted lower network layers and enough interoperable TCP for local clients and servers.
objectives: Trace encapsulation from Ethernet through TCP
Maintain sequence and acknowledgement invariants
Recover from bounded loss, duplicates, and reordering

modules.title: Telnet Server
modules.artifact: A loopback-only concurrent remote shell with two-client and disconnect-cleanup evidence
modules.summary: Use sockets and processes together while keeping the intentionally insecure service isolated from real networks.
objectives: Connect accepted sockets to child process I/O
Reap disconnected sessions without leaking resources
Explain why a bind shell is unsafe outside the test network

modules.title: Dynamic Linking
modules.artifact: A userspace loader that maps one shared library, resolves scoped relocations, and applies final page permissions
modules.summary: Explore how a userspace program can map and relocate shared code without making it a prerequisite for the browser.
objectives: Relate PIC, GOT, PLT, symbols, and runtime relocations
Resolve or reject every imported symbol deterministically
Apply writable-then-executable protections safely

modules.title: Text Web Browser
modules.artifact: A static-link-capable terminal browser that safely parses local HTTP and HTML fixtures and follows links
modules.summary: Complete the vertical slice with bounded URL, DNS, HTTP, HTML, layout, and terminal-rendering behavior.
objectives: Parse hostile network text with explicit limits
Separate fetching, document parsing, layout, and rendering
Navigate deterministic pages without TLS, CSS, or JavaScript

modules.title: JTAG Interface
modules.artifact: A C bit-bang JTAG driver verified against a simulated TAP state machine
modules.summary: Learn the JTAG state machine in simulation before any USB firmware or physical target is involved.
objectives: Drive the TAP through reset and shift states
Read an IDCODE with bit-order evidence
Separate host protocol logic from physical transport

modules.title: FPGA Board
modules.artifact: A reviewed supported-dev-board integration or, separately, a fabrication-ready design package with power-up plan
modules.summary: Use a supported FPGA board for the course; treat custom BGA board design and reflow as a separately supervised EE project.
objectives: Map logical peripherals to documented board resources
Review clocks, reset, power, pin constraints, and voltage compatibility
Apply a current-limited, staged power-up and safety checklist

modules.title: Hardware Bringup
modules.artifact: A reproducible bitstream and staged LED, UART, RAM, storage, and network bringup report
modules.summary: Carry proven simulation checkpoints onto real hardware one subsystem at a time, without weakening the software completion path.
objectives: Build and identify a bitstream reproducibly
Separate electrical, timing, logic, and software failure hypotheses
Compare hardware observations with known simulation traces
